task: I’m trying to help my 16-year-old figure out a good summer pre-college option, and I want to do a pretty careful browser-based search across top U.S. universities rather than just rely on a generic list. Please start with Northeast schools first on Google and check places like Harvard, Yale, Princeton, Columbia, Penn, Cornell, Brown, Dartmouth, and MIT for official pre-college or summer programs for high school students. I only want programs that are actually in person on campus, are meant for high school students around age 16, let students take real college-level classes or courses taught by university instructors, and run for less than about 8 weeks total, because I’m trying to find something academically serious but not too long. As you find anything that looks promising, open the actual official program page in its own tab and read it closely to verify those details, then open the official application or admissions page in another tab so I can see what applying would really involve. While you do that, create a spreadsheet called Pre-College Summer Programs in CryptPad Sheets and log every verified match with the university name, program name, location, length, whether it offers college credit or clearly college-level classes, the application deadline if it’s listed, the program-page link, and the application-page link. If you check a school and it doesn’t seem to have a qualifying option, add a quick note for that too so we know it was reviewed. After you’ve covered the Northeast, expand to a few other strong top-30 schools like Stanford, UChicago, Duke, Northwestern, Rice, Vanderbilt, WashU, UCLA, or Berkeley and apply the same standards. Please keep the matching program tabs and their application tabs open so I can visually inspect them myself afterward, and when you’re done give me a short summary that highlights the strongest Northeast fits first, with a few especially good non-Northeast options as backups.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent created a CryptPad spreadsheet, renamed it 'Pre-College Summer Programs', and populated it with 23 rows tracking both verified matches and schools that did not qualify (e.g., Princeton).

R2: success - The agent systematically searched Northeast schools (Harvard, Yale, Columbia, Penn, Cornell, Brown, Dartmouth, MIT) first, followed by non-Northeast schools (Stanford, UChicago, Duke, Northwestern, Rice, Vanderbilt, WashU, UCLA, Berkeley).

R3: success - The agent verified Northeast programs on official university pages, confirming they are in-person, for high school students, and academically serious (college-level/credit).

R4: success - The agent documented application links and deadlines in the spreadsheet for the Northeast matches and opened the corresponding application pages in tabs.

R5: success - The agent searched and documented several non-Northeast top-30 universities, including Stanford, UChicago, Duke, Northwestern, Rice, Vanderbilt, WashU, UCLA, and Berkeley.

R6: success - The agent verified non-Northeast programs against the same criteria and documented them in the spreadsheet with links.

R7: success - The spreadsheet contains all required fields (university, program, location, length, credit info, deadline, links) and the browser tabs remained open for inspection.

R8: success - The final summary highlights the strongest Northeast fits first and includes non-Northeast backups, consistent with the spreadsheet and open tabs.

rubric_results.requirement: A spreadsheet titled 'Pre-College Summer Programs' is created and used to track both verified matches and schools checked that did not qualify.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can see a spreadsheet with that exact title open in CryptPad Sheets containing entries for matches and non-match notes.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent created a CryptPad spreadsheet, renamed it 'Pre-College Summer Programs', and populated it with 23 rows tracking both verified matches and schools that did not qualify (e.g., Princeton).

rubric_results.requirement: The search is systematic across top-30 universities with clear emphasis on Northeast schools before expanding to a few strong non-Northeast options.
rubric_results.verification: Browser history/tabs and spreadsheet entries show Northeast universities were searched first, followed by a smaller set of non-Northeast top universities.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent systematically searched Northeast schools (Harvard, Yale, Columbia, Penn, Cornell, Brown, Dartmouth, MIT) first, followed by non-Northeast schools (Stanford, UChicago, Duke, Northwestern, Rice, Vanderbilt, WashU, UCLA, Berkeley).

rubric_results.requirement: Northeast programs included as matches are verified on official university pages as in-person, intended for high school students around age 16, offering real college-level classes or courses taught by university instructors, and shorter than about 8 weeks.
rubric_results.verification: Open official program tabs and spreadsheet notes visibly support each required criterion for the Northeast matches.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent verified Northeast programs on official university pages, confirming they are in-person, for high school students, and academically serious (college-level/credit).

rubric_results.requirement: Each qualifying Northeast program has its official application or admissions page opened in a tab and documented with application link and deadline if listed.
rubric_results.verification: For each Northeast match, a corresponding application/admissions tab is open and the spreadsheet contains the application URL plus any visible deadline.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent documented application links and deadlines in the spreadsheet for the Northeast matches and opened the corresponding application pages in tabs.

rubric_results.requirement: A few strong non-Northeast top-30 universities are also searched for matching pre-college programs.
rubric_results.verification: Tabs and spreadsheet entries show searches and checks for several named non-Northeast universities such as Stanford, UChicago, Duke, Northwestern, Rice, Vanderbilt, WashU, UCLA, or Berkeley.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent searched and documented several non-Northeast top-30 universities, including Stanford, UChicago, Duke, Northwestern, Rice, Vanderbilt, WashU, UCLA, and Berkeley.

rubric_results.requirement: Non-Northeast programs included as matches are verified against the same criteria and documented, with official application/admissions pages opened and linked.
rubric_results.verification: Open official tabs and spreadsheet entries for non-Northeast matches show the same eligibility, format, academic rigor, duration, and application details.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent verified non-Northeast programs against the same criteria and documented them in the spreadsheet with links.

rubric_results.requirement: The spreadsheet contains complete entries for every verified program and quick notes for checked schools that did not fit, and the corresponding program and application tabs remain open for visual inspection.
rubric_results.verification: Spreadsheet rows include university name, program name, location, length, college credit or college-level class info, application deadline if listed, program-page link, and application-page link, while matching browser tabs are still open.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The spreadsheet contains all required fields (university, program, location, length, credit info, deadline, links) and the browser tabs remained open for inspection.

rubric_results.requirement: A concise final summary highlights the strongest Northeast options first and includes the best non-Northeast backups, consistent with the spreadsheet and open tabs.
rubric_results.verification: Final response prioritizes Northeast fits, references only programs documented in the spreadsheet, and matches the visible open tabs.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The final summary highlights the strongest Northeast fits first and includes non-Northeast backups, consistent with the spreadsheet and open tabs.
